content.text: Most GenAI programme status reports fail for one of two reasons: they bury the signal in detail (reviewers read every word and leave without knowing if the programme is healthy), or they are so brief they omit the context needed for decisions. A well-designed report template solves both problems by forcing the author to separate
A status report is not a diary. It is a decision-support document. If reading it takes more than four minutes and leaves stakeholders unsure what action is needed, rewrite it.

attrs.alt: Programme status report anatomy: five sections — highlights, risks/blockers, milestone tracker, next-week commitments, decisions needed
attrs.title: Programme Status Report Anatomy

content.text: The five-section structure above is the standard for GenAI programmes at enterprise scale. Sections 1–2 (highlights and risks) cover the past week. Section 3 (milestones) anchors the programme against its schedule baseline. Sections 4–5 (commitments and decisions) orient readers toward what happens next. Every section is fixed-length — authors are forced to prioritise.

Treat the status report as a typed data structure generated from authoritative sources (Jira, risk register, milestone tracker) rather than a blank document filled manually each week. This ensures consistency, enables automation, and makes aggregation at portfolio level tractable.
Manual status reports written from scratch each Friday suffer from recall bias, omissions, and inconsistent format across authors. The preferred pattern is to
generate the report skeleton from data sources
and let the PM add interpretation—not raw facts.

from datetime import date, timedelta
import json

def pull_milestone_statuses(programme_id: str) -> list[MilestoneStatus]:
    """
    In production: query Jira/Linear or the programme's milestone store.
    Here we return static test data.
    """
    return [
        MilestoneStatus("IM-1: Integration Ready",  date(2025,3,7),  date(2025,3,7),  RAGStatus.GREEN,   "J. Smith"),
        MilestoneStatus("IM-2: Release Ready",       date(2025,4,18), date(2025,4,25), RAGStatus.AMBER,   "A. Patel"),
        MilestoneStatus("Go-Live",                   date(2025,5,2),  date(2025,5,2),  RAGStatus.BLOCKED, "A. Patel"),
    ]

def pull_open_risks(programme_id: str) -> list[RiskItem]:
    """Query risk register for open items, sorted by RAG severity."""
    return [
        RiskItem("Vendor API rate limit blocks load test", RAGStatus.RED,   "Platform", "Implement retry + backoff"),
        RiskItem("Data clearance delayed 3 days",          RAGStatus.AMBER, "Data",     "Parallel track for non-PII fields"),
        RiskItem("GDPR review on track for W9 close",      RAGStatus.GREEN, "Legal",    ""),
    ]

def derive_overall_rag(milestones: list[MilestoneStatus], risks: list[RiskItem]) -> RAGStatus:
    """Highest severity across milestones and risks drives the overall RAG."""
    statuses = [m.rag for m in milestones] + [r.rag for r in risks]
    if RAGStatus.BLOCKED in statuses or RAGStatus.RED in statuses:
        return RAGStatus.RED
    if RAGStatus.AMBER in statuses:
        return RAGStatus.AMBER
    return RAGStatus.GREEN

def generate_report_skeleton(programme_id: str, report_date: date) -> WeeklyStatusReport:
    milestones = pull_milestone_statuses(programme_id)
    risks = pull_open_risks(programme_id)
    overall = derive_overall_rag(milestones, risks)
    week = report_date.isocalendar().week

    return WeeklyStatusReport(
        programme=programme_id,
        reporting_week=week,
        report_date=report_date,
        overall_rag=overall,
        owner="Programme Manager",
        # PM fills in highlights and commitments; risks/milestones come from data
        highlights=["[PM: add 3-5 factual highlights for the week]"],
        risks=risks,
        milestones=milestones,
        next_week_commitments=["[PM: add specific commitments with owner and date]"],
        decisions_needed=[],
    )

report = generate_report_skeleton("genai-customer-copilot", date(2025, 2, 28))
errors = report.validate()
if errors:
    print("VALIDATION ERRORS:", errors)
else:
    print(f"[W{report.reporting_week}] {report.programme}: {report.overall_rag_label()}")
    for m in report.milestones:
        print(f"  {m.name}: {m.label()} (owner: {m.owner})")
A good template is necessary but not sufficient. The report only becomes a reliable tool when it is backed by consistent
— recurring practices that turn reporting from a chore into a genuine decision loop.
Jira/risk register frozen for the week

is the critical discipline: if teams can update Jira after the cut-off, the report reflects a moving target and authors spend time chasing late updates instead of writing interpretation. The cut-off also signals to teams that their updates matter — they will be read before end of business.
The same underlying data should produce different views depending on the audience. Engineers need task-level detail; sponsors need one-page summaries with financial indicators.

: overall RAG shows GREEN while individual milestones show AMBER/RED. Derive the overall RAG programmatically from the worst sub-status; never let authors set it manually without overriding the derivation.
: two-paragraph prose explanations of each bullet. Impose a 15-word hard cap per highlight. If something requires more than 15 words to state, it is not a highlight—it is a decision request.

: risks are informational; decisions are actionable. If a risk requires sponsor approval, move it to the Decisions Needed section with a named approver and deadline. Leaving it in the risk list means it will never be acted on.

: last week's commitments not reviewed at Monday standup. Every commitment must be verified green/amber/red at standup before new ones are added. Unreviewed commitments signal that reporting is performative rather than functional.
: a status report published mid-week covers a different observation window than the committed Friday cadence and breaks the portfolio roll-up rhythm. Treat the Friday 17:00 publish as a hard SLA.
At PMO level, individual programme reports are aggregated into a portfolio status. The roll-up should be generated, not written: collect
from each programme, sort by RAG severity, and surface the worst-status programmes first.

from typing import Iterable

def portfolio_rollup(reports: Iterable[WeeklyStatusReport]) -> list[dict]:
    """
    Aggregate programme reports into a portfolio view, worst RAG first.
    """
    rag_order = {
        RAGStatus.BLOCKED: 0,
        RAGStatus.RED:     1,
        RAGStatus.AMBER:   2,
        RAGStatus.GREEN:   3,
    }
    rows = []
    for r in reports:
        errors = r.validate()
        row = render_report(r, ReportAudience.PORTFOLIO)
        row["validation_errors"] = errors
        row["sort_key"] = rag_order.get(r.overall_rag, 99)
        rows.append(row)
    return sorted(rows, key=lambda r: r["sort_key"])


# Example roll-up across three programmes
from datetime import date

reports = [
    WeeklyStatusReport(
        programme="genai-customer-copilot",
        reporting_week=9, report_date=date(2025,2,28),
        overall_rag=RAGStatus.AMBER, owner="PM-A",
        highlights=[], risks=[], milestones=[], next_week_commitments=[], decisions_needed=[],
    ),
    WeeklyStatusReport(
        programme="genai-internal-search",
        reporting_week=9, report_date=date(2025,2,28),
        overall_rag=RAGStatus.GREEN, owner="PM-B",
        highlights=[], risks=[], milestones=[], next_week_commitments=[], decisions_needed=[],
    ),
]
for row in portfolio_rollup(reports):
    print(f"[{row['overall_rag']}] {row['programme']}")

Status reports are only valuable if they trigger action. Define explicit thresholds that convert a RAG status change into a mandatory escalation and a structured remediation cadence. Without thresholds, AMBER lingers indefinitely because no one knows when it must become a formal escalation.
PM notifies delivery director by EOD; risk register updated
Delivery director convenes emergency re-plan session
Escalate to programme board with root cause + options
Mandatory steering committee session; programme review
PM escalates to delivery director; CTO notified if budget

from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import date, timedelta
from enum import Enum

class EscalationTrigger(Enum):
    RAG_AMBER_FIRST    = "rag_amber_first"
    RAG_RED_FIRST      = "rag_red_first"
    AMBER_PERSISTS     = "amber_persists_3w"
    RED_PERSISTS       = "red_persists_2w"
    DECISION_OVERDUE   = "decision_overdue"

@dataclass
class EscalationEvent:
    programme: str
    trigger: EscalationTrigger
    detected_on: date
    required_action: str
    deadline: date
    owner: str
    resolved: bool = False

def check_escalations(
    reports: list[WeeklyStatusReport],
) -> list[EscalationEvent]:
    """
    Scan the last N weekly reports for escalation triggers.
    Reports must be ordered oldest → newest.
    """
    events: list[EscalationEvent] = []
    if not reports:
        return events

    latest = reports[-1]
    prog = latest.programme
    today = latest.report_date

    # Amber persists: last 3 consecutive reports all AMBER or worse
    if len(reports) >= 3:
        last3 = [r.overall_rag for r in reports[-3:]]
        if all(r in (RAGStatus.AMBER, RAGStatus.RED, RAGStatus.BLOCKED) for r in last3):
            events.append(EscalationEvent(
                programme=prog,
                trigger=EscalationTrigger.AMBER_PERSISTS,
                detected_on=today,
                required_action="Escalate to programme board with root cause + options",
                deadline=today + timedelta(days=7),
                owner="Delivery Director",
            ))

    # Red persists: last 2 consecutive reports RED or BLOCKED
    if len(reports) >= 2:
        last2 = [r.overall_rag for r in reports[-2:]]
        if all(r in (RAGStatus.RED, RAGStatus.BLOCKED) for r in last2):
            events.append(EscalationEvent(
                programme=prog,
                trigger=EscalationTrigger.RED_PERSISTS,
                detected_on=today,
                required_action="Mandatory steering committee within 5 business days",
                deadline=today + timedelta(days=5),
                owner="CTO / Programme Sponsor",
            ))

    # Overdue decisions
    for d in latest.decisions_needed:
        if d.deadline < today:
            events.append(EscalationEvent(
                programme=prog,
                trigger=EscalationTrigger.DECISION_OVERDUE,
                detected_on=today,
                required_action=f"Decision overdue: {d.description}",
                deadline=today,
                owner=d.approver,
            ))

    return events

The most scalable status reporting setups automate data ingestion so that the PM's job is interpretation and decisions—not data collection. The following patterns work across common enterprise stacks.

import requests
from datetime import date

JIRA_BASE = "https://yourorg.atlassian.net"

def fetch_milestone_epics(project_key: str, token: str) -> list[MilestoneStatus]:
    """
    Query Jira for epics labelled 'integration-milestone' and map to MilestoneStatus.
    Requires a Jira API token with read access to the project.
    """
    url = f"{JIRA_BASE}/rest/api/3/search"
    jql = (
        f'project={project_key} AND issuetype=Epic '
        f'AND labels="integration-milestone" AND statusCategory!=Done'
    )
    resp = requests.get(
        url,
        params={"jql": jql, "fields": "summary,duedate,status,assignee,priority"},
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Accept": "application/json"},
        timeout=10,
    )
    resp.raise_for_status()
    milestones = []
    for issue in resp.json().get("issues", []):
        fields = issue["fields"]
        rag = _jira_priority_to_rag(fields.get("priority", {}).get("name", "Medium"))
        target = date.fromisoformat(fields["duedate"]) if fields.get("duedate") else date.today()
        milestones.append(MilestoneStatus(
            name=fields["summary"],
            target_date=target,
            forecast_date=target,   # override if you store forecast in a custom field
            rag=rag,
            owner=(fields.get("assignee") or {}).get("displayName", "Unassigned"),
        ))
    return milestones

def _jira_priority_to_rag(priority: str) -> RAGStatus:
    mapping = {"Highest": RAGStatus.RED, "High": RAGStatus.AMBER,
               "Medium": RAGStatus.GREEN, "Low": RAGStatus.GREEN}
    return mapping.get(priority, RAGStatus.GREEN)
Programme status reports for GenAI initiatives should be
(generated from Jira, risk registers, and milestone stores),
(the Decisions Needed section is the most important one), and

(executive views strip detail; portfolio views strip narrative). The five-section template — highlights, risks, milestones, commitments, decisions — provides a consistent scaffold that survives team turnover and scales from a single programme to a portfolio of twenty.
